name: Google Hotels Lookup
description: Search for hotels via Google Search and extract the visible Hotels widget text (best-effort).

browser_automation.description: Uses google.com/search?q=hotels+QUERY because the full Hotels SPA at google.com/travel/hotels is too brittle.

sequences.name: google_hotels_search_extract
sequences.description: Open a hotels search panel and dump the result panel text.

required_variables.name: search_query
required_variables.description: Location and/or hotel keywords (e.g. 'San Francisco', 'Paris hotels near Louvre'). Aliases: query, q.

steps.name: Navigate to Google Search hotels query

help.summary: Look up hotels via Google Search and return the visible widget text. Best-effort.

parameters.name: search_query
parameters.description: Location and/or hotel keywords. Aliases query and q work too.
parameters.example: San Francisco hotels near Union Square

examples.command: rzn-browser run google hotels --param search_query="San Francisco hotels near Union Square"
examples.description: Search for SF hotels with a location anchor.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google hotels --param query="boutique hotel Lisbon Alfama"
examples.description: Use the query alias with a neighborhood + style filter.

help.returns: Object with one field, search_text — inner text of the #search panel including the Hotels widget. The caller is responsible for parsing prices/ratings/names because Google rotates the layout.
notes: No dates / occupancy params here — defaults to whatever the widget shows.
If you need date/occupancy filters, drive google.com/travel/hotels directly via orchestrator.
